_readme.purpose: Answer key for the EXTRACTION eval (scripts/extraction_eval.py). Each entry pins the correct STRUCTURE of one specific table in the indexed library so we can measure whether find_tables / the reconstructors captured it faithfully. Seeded across multiple books and table types (cross-ref lookups, multi-language references, weapon/vehicle stat blocks, roll-result tables, career skill grids, rank ladders, equipment lists) so the accuracy number reflects real cross-book performance, not one book. Each correction also becomes a labelled example for a future learned scanner (see the extraction-roadmap project memory).
_readme.how_to_score: Find the cached table by `match_cells` (cell values that identify it; the stated `page` disambiguates when several tables share those cells), then check `expect_headers` against the header row, `expect_row_labels` against the first column of the data rows (include the LAST label to guard the trailing-row drop), `min_rows`, and optional `max_rows`. A table is correct only if all hold. `max_rows` catches OVER-capture (two tables merged into one, stray lines pulled in) that a min-only check would pass.
_readme.expect_cells: Optional list of externally-grounded cell-VALUE assertions, each `{row, col, value}`: locate the column by its header (`col`) and the data row by its first-cell label (`row`), then require the intersecting cell to equal `value` (normalized). Shape checks (headers/labels/row-count) can pass while a value is wrong — a column shear, a transposed or dropped cell. These pin the truth against the system's authority (see each entry's `external_source`: Archives of Nethys for PF2e, the Traveller SRD, etc.), so a mis-extraction that keeps the right shape still fails. Values are stored here once (verified against the source) so we don't re-fetch — see the external-source-fetch-rate-limits note. EDITION CAVEAT: a source may track a different edition than the indexed book (e.g. traveller-srd.com is original Mongoose, this Traveller book is the 2022 update), so ground ONLY edition-stable values and record the divergences in an `external_note`; grounding an edition-changed value would wrongly fail a correct extraction.
_readme.two_numbers: summarize() reports `overall_accuracy` over ALL tables (the realistic cross-book picture — known_broken tables count as the failures they are) and `gate_accuracy` over only the known-good tables (the regression guard that exits non-zero below --threshold). When an extraction fix lands, flip `known_broken` to false so the table joins the gate.
_readme.local_only: Reads cache/*.json (the copyrighted books can't live in the repo), so it's a LOCAL guard like scripts/retrieval_eval.py. To validate an extraction fix: re-extract (python -m lorehound.index, or /reindex), then re-run the eval.
books_covered: Traveller — Aliens of Charted Space Vol 2 (untuned by our reconstructors; surfaces real failures)
Pathfinder 2e core (a new system + heavy Paizo styling; a deliberate stress test — most tables use shading not ruling lines, so find_tables misses them)

entries.title: Chance of Success, Pushed Roll
entries.what_was_wrong: FIXED (find-tables-lines-v11): find_tables(strategy=lines) dropped the bottom D12 attribute/skill row (no bottom ruling line). The trailing-row recovery pass restores it: ['D12','88%','93%','96%','97%'].

entries.title: Chance of Success, Initial Roll
entries.what_was_wrong: FIXED (find-tables-lines-v11): same D12 bottom-row drop (recovered ['D12','65%','74%','79%','82%']). Extracted title is still garbled ('shows the chance when pushing.') — title-only, rows are complete.

entries.title: Military Ranks
entries.what_was_wrong: The full US/Soviet/Polish/Swedish ladder chargen promotions read. Trailing-row recovery also restored the top 'General' rank (19->20 rows).

entries.title: Critical Injuries — Arms
entries.what_was_wrong: Regression guard for a known-good D10 1-10 roll table with wrapped multi-line EFFECTS cells.

entries.title: Soviet Military Vehicles (tank roster)
entries.what_was_wrong: Trailing-row recovery regression guard: find_tables captured only 3 of 8 vehicles; T-55/T-62/T-64/T-72/T-80 sit below the last ruling line and were dropped. All 8 must be present. (This block is headerless — its first row is data, so the scorer reads row 0 as header; row labels are checked from the rest.)

entries.title: Terrain Movement & Encounter Distance
entries.what_was_wrong: Wide table whose bottom ENCOUNTER DISTANCE row sat below the last ruling line (recovered by trailing-row pass).

entries.title: Navy — Skills and Training (Table A)
entries.what_was_wrong: Geometrically-reconstructed Table A (the universal skill grid) with the commissioned-track Officer column. find_tables shatters these; the reconstructor must yield the 7-row grid with all four columns.

entries.title: Merchant — Ranks and Bonuses
entries.what_was_wrong: Reconstructed career rank track, ranks 0-6 with skill/bonus per rank.

entries.title: Agent — Mishaps
entries.what_was_wrong: Roll | wrapped-paragraph table (1D, six outcomes). Each description wraps over many lines; the reconstructor keys rows by the roll index.

entries.title: Artist — Events
entries.what_was_wrong: 2D events table, 11 outcomes (2-12) plus header; wrapped-paragraph cells.

entries.title: Mustering Out Benefits
entries.what_was_wrong: 1D | CASH | BENEFITS mustering table (rolls 1-7 incl. the DM-boosted 7th row).

entries.title: Explosives (weapon stats)
entries.what_was_wrong: Equipment stat table with a 9-column header (incl. TRAITS); guards against dropped columns/rows.
entries.external_note: EDITION CAVEAT: traveller-srd.com is the ORIGINAL Mongoose edition (damage in 'd6', area as 'Radius Nd6 metres'), NOT the Mongoose 2022 update in this book (damage in 'D', area as the 'Blast X' trait). So only EDITION-STABLE values are grounded here: TL (Plastic 6, TDX/Pocket Nuke 12) and Plastic/TDX damage+cost (SRD 3d6/4d6 ≡ this book's 3D/4D; costs Cr200/Cr1000 identical). Pocket Nuke's damage (6DD vs 2d6×20) and cost (Cr250000 vs Cr20000) and the Blast traits diverge between editions — NOT asserted, since grounding them against this SRD would wrongly fail a correct 2022 extraction.

entries.system: Traveller — Aliens of Charted Space Vol 2
entries.title: Worker — Ranks and Bonuses
entries.what_was_wrong: Known-good rank ladder in an untuned supplement (RANK | TITLE | SKILL OR BONUS, 0-6).

entries.system: Traveller — Aliens of Charted Space Vol 2
entries.title: Mortarbot (robot stat block)
entries.what_was_wrong: Robot stat card: header line + a stat row + SKILLS/ATTACKS/TRAITS/PROGRAMMING label rows.

entries.system: Traveller — Aliens of Charted Space Vol 2
entries.title: Career — Skills and Training (Table A)
entries.what_was_wrong: FIXED. Table A (universal skills) and the specialist-skills Table B were MERGED into one 14-row table (a mid-table '1D | FIELD AGENT | ...' header) because no career reconstructor fires on this supplement. The generic split-stacked-roll-tables pass now separates them at the second die-label header into a clean 7-row Table A.

entries.system: Traveller — Aliens of Charted Space Vol 2
entries.title: Leader — Mishaps
entries.what_was_wrong: FIXED. The 1D Mishaps and 2D Events tables were MERGED into one 20-row table. The split pass separates them at the '2D | EVENT' header and trims the stray 'EVENTS TABLE' banner, leaving a clean 7-row Mishaps table.

entries.system: Traveller — Aliens of Charted Space Vol 2
entries.title: Leader — Events
entries.what_was_wrong: The 2D Events half of the merged Mishaps+Events table, now its own clean 12-row table after the split pass (rolls 2-12).

entries.title: Table 1-2: The Nine Alignments
entries.what_was_wrong: Known-good 3x3 alignment grid — proof a clean ruled grid extracts fine even in this stylized book.

entries.title: Table 1-1: Ability Modifiers
entries.what_was_wrong: FIXED. The book prints this as two side-by-side Score|Modifier halves, double-struck for faux-bold — so extraction read every cell twice ('10–11 +0 10–11 +0'). Fix: de-dup same-position words, then detect the repeated column group and unroll the two halves into one clean Ability Score | Modifier table (1 to 24–25). Values match the official PF2e progression (floor((score-10)/2): 10→+0, 24-25→+7), confirmed via Archives of Nethys.

entries.title: Martial Weapons (Equipment)
entries.what_was_wrong: FIXED by Stage A + B. Stage A recovered this table (was missing entirely — Paizo's shaded weapons table has no ruling lines for find_tables/lines). Stage B's geometric reconstruction (picked over text-strategy by the lower fragment score) gives clean columns — Damage/Bulk/Hands/Group are now each their own column (no more 'Bu'/'lk' split), so the header check passes. (p282 Martial Weapons: Longsword 1d8 S Bulk 1, Maul 1d12 B Bulk 2, …) Cross-checked vs Archives of Nethys (Longsword 1d8 S, Bulk 1, Hands 1, Group Sword — exact match).

entries.title: Simple Weapons (Melee)
entries.what_was_wrong: Recovered by Stage A + B (shaded table). Clean columns: Club 1d6 B / Bulk 1 / Group Club; Dagger 1d4 P / Bulk L / Group Knife. The column header isn't row 0 (an Unarmed/section banner precedes it) — the eval finds the best-matching header row. Cross-checked vs Archives of Nethys.

entries.title: Table 3-17: Sorcerer Spells Per Day
entries.what_was_wrong: Recovered by Stage A + B (shaded grid). Clean 12-column grid: character Level 1-20 × Cantrips + spell levels 1st-10th (level 1 = 5 cantrips, 3 first-level slots; level 3 = 5/4/3). Title and 'Spell Level' banner rows precede the column header (handled by best-row header detection). Cross-checked vs Archives of Nethys (Sorcerer).
